title: Agedashi Tofu Crispy Dashi

seo.title: Agedashi Tofu Recipe: Crispy Dashi Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Agedashi Tofu! Learn to make perfectly crispy fried tofu in a rich, savory dashi broth. This authentic Japanese vegetarian recipe is easy & delicious.
seo.h1: Agedashi Tofu: Crispy & Authentic Japanese Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Agedashi Tofu
Pro Tips for Crispy Agedashi Tofu
seo.imageAlt: A plate of golden crispy Agedashi Tofu served in a warm dashi broth, garnished with grated daikon and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in this classic Japanese delight: perfectly crispy Agedashi Tofu bathed in a flavorful dashi broth, a true vegetarian treat.

description: Experience the delicate balance of textures and flavors with this authentic Agedashi Tofu. Golden-fried crispy tofu is gently immersed in a warm, savory dashi broth, creating a comforting and elegant vegetarian dish. This recipe promises a delightful culinary journey into traditional Japanese cuisine.

ingredients.notes: pressed and cut into 1.5 inch cubes

instructions.title: Press the Tofu
instructions.text: Drain the tofu blocks and wrap them in several layers of paper towels or a clean kitchen towel. Place a heavy object on top (like a cutting board with cans) and press for at least 30 minutes to remove excess water. This step is crucial for crispy tofu. Once pressed, cut the tofu into 1.5-inch cubes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetarian Dashi Broth
instructions.text: In a pot, combine 4 cups of water, the kombu, and dried shiitake mushrooms. Let it soak for at least 30 minutes, or ideally overnight in the refrigerator. Gently heat the pot over medium-low heat. Just before it reaches a boil, remove the kombu. Continue to simmer the shiitake for another 10-15 minutes. Remove the shiitake (you can slice and add them back later or use them for another dish). Strain the dashi to ensure it is clear.

instructions.title: Make the Dashi Sauce
instructions.text: To the prepared vegetarian dashi broth, add the soy sauce, mirin, and sugar (if using). Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Keep the sauce warm while you prepare the tofu.

instructions.title: Coat and Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot or wok to 350-360°F (175-180°C). While the oil heats, lightly coat each tofu cube evenly with potato starch, shaking off any excess. Fry the tofu in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot, ensuring the oil temperature remains consistent. Fry for 3-5 minutes per batch, or until golden brown and crispy on all sides.

instructions.title: Drain and Assemble
instructions.text: Carefully remove the fried tofu from the oil with a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Repeat with remaining tofu. Arrange the crispy tofu cubes neatly into individual serving bowls.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Pour the warm dashi sauce over the tofu in each bowl. Garnish generously with grated daikon radish and chopped green onions. Serve immediately while the tofu is still crispy and the broth is warm. Enjoy this classic Japanese vegetarian delight.

tips: For extra crispy tofu, ensure you press out as much water as possible. A well-pressed tofu will absorb less oil and achieve a superior crisp exterior.
Use a good quality Japanese soy sauce and mirin for the most authentic flavor in your dashi broth. The balance of sweet, savory, and umami is key.
Serve Agedashi Tofu immediately after assembling. The crispy exterior of the tofu will soften quickly in the warm dashi broth, so prompt serving is essential for the best texture experience.
article: Agedashi Tofu Crispy Dashi is a beloved Japanese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
